{{Infobox territory
| name = Ragnarus the Crimson Vault
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[Ragnarus the Crimson Vault Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus, also known as the [[Crimson Vault]], is an ancient [[Territory]] and [[Incarnation]]. It serves as a repository for incredibly powerful weapons, though every artifact contained within demands a significant cost from its user. Access to the [[Vault]] is restricted; only those of the [[Damasca]] royal bloodline can open its [[Gates]], and a specific pact limits entry to certain individuals. [[The Territory]] is characterized by its bloody color and the presence of specialized trees that are interconnected across different [[Overlords]]. These trees require regular blood sacrifices to maintain the seal on the [[Incarnations]], a process that has recently shown signs of instability. Those capable of navigating Ragnarus are known as Ragnarus [[Travelers]]. While these individuals can wield immense power—such as the ability to manipulate fire or achieve superhuman strength—the use of Ragnarus artifacts often results in physical degradation or the rapid depletion of one's [[life]] force. [[The Territory]] itself is described as having a pulsing, living quality, and its gates are guarded by entities intended to repel uninvited visitors. Historically, the first [[King of Damasca]] utilized his status as a [[Traveler]] to seal away a destructive force within this realm.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Ragnarus the Crimson Vault]] is a [[Territory]] exclusive to the [[Kings of Damasca]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10">''House of Blades'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]]'s orders from the capital were passed through her [[Territory]] of Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The sacrifice is a function of Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Ragnarus is also known as the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|As one of the original nine [[Territories]], Ragnarus is a location illuminated by torches and a [[ruby]] light that possesses a grasping presence similar to a disembodied heart. This territory, which maintains a direct route into the royal palace of [[Cana]] and is one of the four [[Incarnations]] identified by [[Indirial]], is characterized by its promotion of sacrifice and a requirement that its bloodline remains in power. Within its silver doors marked by the visage of a one-eyed, bearded king, powerful weapons and artifacts reside that always come with a price. During her appointment, [[Leah]] traveled to the territory where Ragnarus witnessed her oath and provided her materialized crown. While [[Kai]] and [[Indirial]] sought its aid, a [[sword]] bearing the name Ragnarus bit [[Master Kai]], and [[Alin]] once stood on the verge of stepping into the realm.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] was once a tree from Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is one of the original nine [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is the ninth [[Territory]] whose only [[Travelers]] are [[Damascan]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus promotes sacrifice.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus contains powerful weapons.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus has silver doors marked with the visage of a bearded, one-eyed king.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The bloodline of Ragnarus must continue and remain in power.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] Traveled to Ragnarus on the day of her appointment.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus witnessed [[Leah]]'s oath.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is a [[Territory]] that has a direct route into the royal palace of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]] and [[Indirial]] sought the aid of Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]]'s crown materializes from Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is a location containing swords.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is the name of the [[sword]] that bit [[Master Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] was on the verge of stepping into Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is a location where artifacts come with a price.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus has a [[ruby]] light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is one of the four [[Incarnations]] identified by [[Indirial]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Lirial]] believes something is from Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus has a sticky, grasping presence like a disembodied heart.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Ragnarus is a place with torches.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|As a [[Territory]] defined by overt violence and instant gratification, Ragnarus reigns over all other [[Territories]]. This entity possesses a poison that marks bodies and maintains an armory of thirsty, glittering weapons fueled by harvested power. Within its domain, Ragnarus holds an antechamber containing a carved wooden throne and is linked to various artifacts, including [[Simon's mask]]. Though [[Leah]] is its last remaining child and can summon the entity using her crown, Ragnarus has directly confronted figures like [[Kai]] and [[Simon]]. Ultimately, the entity is identified as the power of which [[Zakareth]] is the [[Incarnation]], a connection confirmed when [[Kai]] identified a pulsing red metal heart pulled from the [[Tartarus Incarnation]] as Ragnarus.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus possesses a poison that leaves marks on bodies.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c1">''City of Light'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus can harvest power to fuel its armory of thirsty weapons.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus reigns over all other [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is the last remaining child of Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus has an antechamber with a carved wooden throne.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus and [[Elysia]] once fought.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]]'s blade is a red-and-black [[sword]] that [[Lycus]] believes comes from Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus makes matters more direct.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is an entity that crippled [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is a power or entity that [[Leah]] can summon using her crown.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is a [[Territory]] of overt violence and instant gratification.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The mask]] [[Simon]] wears was originally an artifact of Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus has many tools that can be used as weapons.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is a different [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus may prevent [[Denner]] from banishing and re-summoning his [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A red, pulsing metal heart was pulled from the [[Tartarus Incarnation]], which [[Kai]] identified as Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is the entity of which [[Zakareth]] is the [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is a place with glittering weapons.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Ragnarus is described as being straightforward.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] went blade-to-blade with Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"Leah pushed her bleeding hand to the silver doors of Ragnarus. The doors, marked with the visage of the bearded, one-eyed king, swung silently open at her offering."<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“How about a sword from Ragnarus?” Simon asked.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“Your brother’s sword has quite the bite,” he said. ... “Ragnarus. Well, don’t worry about it.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“That’s four,” he said. “Endross, Ornheim, Naraka…and Ragnarus.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“Whatever it is, it’s almost certainly from Ragnarus,” Grandmaster Lirial concluded.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Territories]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
